title: Ports <1024 are unreachable from outside the host
body:
### Issue Description

When running a rootless container that publishes "privileged" ports (aka, ports <1024) are unreachable from other devices. Specifically, ports 80, 81, 443 and 500 where tested.

Even after adding `net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start=80` to sysclt config, which lets 
my user bind to these ports.

Reaching these ports works just fine over localhost, but not from another device on the same LAN.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Allow user to bind to ports <1024 with sysctl
2. Create a podman rootless container that publishes a port <1024
```sh
# For example:
$ podman run -p 80:80 nginx:1.29.0-alpine
```
3. Reach said port by navigating to LAN IP from another device

I have also tried pasta and slirp4netns, both present the same issue

### Describe the results you received

When the exposed port is <1024, iOS Firefox returns NSURLDomainError, iOS Safari "Can't connect to server", Firefox and Edge on Windows 11 "Connection Timeout"

If the exposed port is instead <1024, such as 8080, it is perfectly reachable across LAN

### Describe the results you expected

Ports <1024 and ports >1024 behave the same if the OS allows the user to bind to those ports

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I am running pretty much stock Fedora 42 Workstation. I originally attributed this to Tailscale VPN, then to DNS issues, and as I slowly peeled back layer of this "onion" I am left with nothing but Podman itself as the source of the issue.


### Additional information

On the multiple Reddit threads I have asked for help, it's come down to pasta/slirp4netns issues. I can't say for certain, because I only briefly have read what these are, but clearly something about the rootless networking does NOT like ports <1024.

For a LOT more context that probably is not relevant, here's the two Reddit posts I made: [Post 1](https://www.reddit.com/r/podman/comments/1lxklcf/networking_rootless_podman_containers/) (I still thought Tailscale was part of the issue) [Post 2](https://www.reddit.com/r/podman/comments/1ly85b1/cant_route_to_priviledged_ports_exposed_through/) (At this point I already knew the issue existed also within LAN so Tailscale was not involved)

comments.body:
It was in fact, not Podman

I am so sorry for wasting anyone's time. After 2-3 days debugging this issue I thought I had tried everything. In fact, I had tried the solution before, but I continued testing afterwards for some reason, I guess I didn't properly check because I almost immediately undid the solution (as I'm seeing in my zsh history).

It was a firewall issue
